description_long: This house features a long rectangular structure with light gray brick walls and a prominent red-tiled gable roof. The front facade includes a single wooden door flanked by two blue-framed windows on each side, creating a balanced and simple appearance. The side walls are windowless, emphasizing the elongated form of the building. Both shorter ends of the house have two evenly spaced windows, providing natural light to the interior. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with small, uniformly arranged terracotta tiles. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function.
description: A long, rectangular house with light gray brick walls, a red-tiled gable roof, and blue-framed windows. The front features a central wooden door and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: End view showing two blue-framed windows and the steeply pitched red-tiled roof.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the central wooden door and four blue-framed windows symmetrically arranged.
description_view_2: Opposite end view with two blue-framed windows and the gable roof.
description_view_3: Rear view with a plain brick wall and no windows or doors.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with no steps, porch, or decorative elements, flanked by windows.
